summary: During TMJ surgery via arthroscopy, functional limitations might arise from internal adhesions which require careful removal using a specific instrument; among various options discussed, Ho:YAG laser stands out as ideal because of its ability to cut precisely while minimizing thermal injury, crucially important when working close to vital anatomical landmarks such as nerves supplying face muscles. Agent1 reasoned based primarily on the need for an extremely controlled method capable of safely severing problematic scarred connective tissues within confined spaces typical during TMJ procedures involving intricate bony articulations and associated soft-tissue interfaces prone to scarring post-injury or inflammation episodes necessitating corrective interventions aimed at restoring normal jaw
